title: completeXConversion allows stealing funds from cross-chain transfers
reason: The BancorNetwork code queries `_bancorX.getXTransferAmount(_conversionId, msg.sender)`, so this contract does not itself authorize a caller to claim someone else's transfer. The supported issue here is a broken funding flow, not a demonstrated front-runnable theft path.

title: updateRegistry allows unauthorized registry modification
reason: `updateRegistry()` does not let callers choose an arbitrary registry; it only follows the current registry's `CONTRACT_REGISTRY` pointer. Permissionless update appears to be an intended sync mechanism, not a direct attacker-controlled hijack from this code alone.

title: No validation that affiliate account can receive tokens
reason: The affiliate fee is paid with an ERC20 transfer, which does not require a payable fallback or token-receiver hook. A bad affiliate address would at most cause the caller's own transaction to revert; it is not a protocol-level vulnerability.

title: Approve race condition in ensureAllowance
reason: The allowance being changed belongs to BancorNetwork itself and is granted only to the designated converter or BancorX contract. The generic ERC20 approve race does not create an independent attacker path here without already assuming a malicious spender.

title: Missing zero-address check for affiliate account
reason: `convertByPath` already treats `address(0)` as affiliate-fee disabled and explicitly requires `_affiliateFee == 0` in that case, so affiliate fees cannot be burned to the zero address through this path.

title: Deprecated functions lack input validation
reason: The deprecated wrappers all route into `convertByPath`, which itself enforces `greaterThanZero(_minReturn)`, so `_minReturn == 0` is still rejected.
